payload.title: Prevent accidental Narada proper task-substrate mutation from local Site work
payload.summary: During local YASB/Narada Site work, the agent misinterpreted a request to inspect Narada machinery in the WSL clone as authorization to mutate Narada proper task state. It ran narada task allocate --count 6, advancing local SQLite task_number_sequence.last_allocated from 972 to 978. The operator remediated by resetting the sequence to 972; no task files or reservations for 973-978 existed, guard passed, and git status was clean.

incident.accidental_effect: Advanced ignored local SQLite task_number_sequence.last_allocated from 972 to 978
remediation: Verified git status is clean: main...origin/main

payload.root_cause: The agent crossed from reading Narada proper doctrine/tooling into mutating Narada proper task lifecycle because the target work substrate was not explicitly confirmed before invoking a mutating task command.
proposed_prevention: Classify task allocate, task create, chapter init, task close, task confirm, inbox triage, inbox promote, and related lifecycle commands as mutating even when preparatory.
Require explicit target locus and path before any Arc/Chapter/Task/Inbox mutation: Narada proper, User Site, PC Site, client Site, data Site, or external repo.
Default ~/src/narada to read-only doctrine/tool inspection unless the operator explicitly says modify Narada proper or names Narada proper as the target work substrate.
Before mutating Narada proper task state, require the agent to state the target locus, repo path, exact mutation command, and why Narada proper owns the work.
Prefer read-only or dry-run commands in Narada proper when the work being planned belongs to a local Site or sidecar repository.
Record this guard in agent-facing instructions so future agents do not use Narada proper task machinery as the task substrate for local Site/YASB operations.

suggested_target.area: agent execution contract / task-governance locus guards
suggested_target.reason: The failure mode can silently mutate ignored local SQLite state without git-visible evidence, so prevention must be instruction-level or command-level, not memory-only.
